facts.text: San Francisco got its name from Mission San Francisco de Asís, which was renamed in 1847 from Yerba Buena, meaning 'good herb'.
The city’s cable cars, a National Historic Landmark, still glide along steep streets, a living reminder of 19th‑century engineering.
Founded in 1860, the Olympic Club is the oldest athletic club in the United States, still hosting elite sports today.
The Fillmore, rebuilt in the 1960s, launched the San Francisco Sound with early shows by Grateful Dead and Janis Joplin.
Bank of America’s 1969 skyscraper at 555 California Street once symbolized middle‑class banking, before the bank moved its headquarters to Charlotte.
The San Francisco Armory, Palace of Fine Arts, and Haas–Lilienthal House are among dozens of landmarks listed on the National Register.
